Petition For Order: (1) Suspending And Removing Latonya Carpenter As Successor Co-Trustee; (2) Requiring An Accounting; And (3) Awarding And Surcharging Respondent For Damages, Double Damages And Interest
Off Calendar Mar-30-2026 Continued To May-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Robert Cross for petitioners Prince Edmonds III, Gabrielle Edmonds, Jason Edmonds, Jermaine Edmonds, and Darvell Edmonds.
Jonathan Madison for respondent La Tonya Carpenter, who was also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Suspend and Remove Successor Co-Trustee, Require an Accounting, and for Surcharge.

The Court inquired regarding the status of the matter and whether progress had been made in addressing the withdrawals identified in the petition. Mr. Madison stated that the parties had communicated and were willing to meet and confer regarding an accounting and supporting documentation.

Mr. Cross argued that Ms. Carpenter made unilateral withdrawals without the required approval of the co-trustee, in violation of the prior court order requiring joint action. Mr. Madison responded that Ms. Carpenter disclosed the transactions, that the withdrawals were proper, and that she did not take more than she was entitled to. Ms. Carpenter addressed the Court and stated that the distributions were consistent with her understanding and discussions with the co-trustee.

The Court stated that the issues regarding whether withdrawals were unauthorized and whether the amounts were proper would need to be litigated and could not be resolved at this time and declined to suspend respondent. The Court ordered that no action be taken on behalf of the trust without written approval of both co-trustees.

The Court directed the parties to meet and confer and exchange information. The matter was continued to May 6, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Michael B. McNaughton; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

The court points to how similar the Joint Case Management Statements submitted by the parties up to this point have been which is not compliant with the court's Case Management Order. The court summarizes what exactly it expects from the Joint Case Management Statements and that up to now, there have been no substantive responses included in the statements. Messrs. Madison and Cross report on some of the progress made via mediation with Neil Bardack with Mr. Cross reporting that he believes that at the very least, there may be a structure for settlement. Mr. Madison reports that perhaps Mr. Bardack may be open to facilitating a binding arbitration. After additional discussion, the court advises that it is not in the position to order the parties to arbitration. Judge Quinn discloses that Neil Bardack and he are former law partners. The court reminds the parties that the complicated nature of this case should inspire the parties to continue to meet and confer regarding settlement and of the importance of submitting a comprehensive, substantive joint trial plan before the impending date for trial setting.

The competing petitions in this matter are continued to Jun-15-2022 at 9:00 am. The Case Management Hearings and Status of Mediation are taken off calendar.

Mr. Madison further stated that he estimates trial would take 4-5 days, that discovery is not a matter of contention, that Prince Edmonds will be deposed on 1/3/20 and his client has also been subpoenaed to be deposed on 1/6/20, that the trial date for the civil case is set for 2/10/20 with an MSC in Mid-January. Additionally, Mr. Madison requested a court order for production of receipts that mortgage has been paid.

The Status on filing of accounting and Petition of beneficiary to compel accounting by trustee, etc. are continued to January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for trial setting if needed. Counsel to meet and confer re: status on mortgage payment, file joint status report by January 17, 2020 stating which claims in petition can be resolved without a hearing, claims that need to be tried, witnesses, times estimate for each witness, and overall time estimate.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney, Robert Cross to appear on January 27, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why he failed to appear.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Robles. Not reported.

Petition Of Beneficiary To Compel Accounting By Trustee, To Compel Trustee To Redress Any Losses To The Trust Caused By Trustee'S Breach(Es) Of Fiducc
Off Calendar Jul-29-2019 Continued To Sep-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jonathan Madison for and with La Tonya Carpenter (Petitioner).
Robert Cross for Prince Edmonds (Respondent).

The Court stated that it seems like there is a fair amount of overlap with the civil case. Mr. Fried stated that there is some overlap and some differences, that there is some urgency, that the mortgage has not been paid for almost a year, that there is concern that the trust bills have not been paid and won't be paid, and that one of the Attorneys expressed that the Trustee lacks capacity to administer the trust. Mr. Cross stated that he believes they have been paying the mortgage, that Prince is being assisted by his Son and his Daughter-in-Law with the finances, that Prince lives in one house with no income coming in, that one house breaks even and that Ms. Carpenter's Mother took money, that there is no trust corpus, and that he will move to consolidate and to have Prince's Son appointed as a Co-Trustee. Mr. Fried spoke of the trust terms, stated that La Tonya is the Successor Trustee, that there is no proof that La Tonya's Mother had taken any money and that there is no evidence of the mortgage being paid. Mr. Cross stated that he has subpoenaed bank records, that it is the only discovery he has done so far, and that once he gets a better handle on the financial records he will do written discovery and depositions. Mr. Fried stated that he might do some discovery, that Mr. Edmonds received an insurance policy and could have paid the mortgage, and that he would like to have this petition granted because of the urgency with the mortgage. Mr. Cross stated that he can provide information on the mortgage status. The Court suggested the Parties meet and confer to frame this case for resolution. Mr. Fried stated that an accounting has not been provided, that there is a bypass trust, that the trust is still holding on to trust property that belongs to his Client and that there are several of hundreds of thousands of dollars that belong to Ms. Carpenter's Mother. Mr. Cross stated that there are two properties that are illiquid, that the only income Prince has is his retirement and social security, that there was an elder abuse claim filed and that they entered into an agreement. The Petition to Compel Accounting by Trustee, etc. is continued to September 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of the accounting is also set for September 30th. The Parties are to meet and confer and come up with a discovery plan and come back with a realistic date for a hearing and an estimated time of the hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
